text: Patients with idh1_mutation=1 have a higher objective_response rate than patients with idh1_mutation=0.
rationale: IDH1 mutations are well-established AML markers with FDA-approved targeted therapy (ivosidenib) and known response associations in guidelines.

text: Patients with npm1_mutation=1 have a higher objective_response rate than patients with npm1_mutation=0 (NPM1 is a favorable AML marker).
rationale: NPM1 mutation as a favorable AML risk marker is standard ELN/NCCN guideline knowledge.

text: Patients with tp53_mutation=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with tp53_mutation=0 (TP53 is an unfavorable marker).
rationale: TP53 mutation as an adverse prognostic and predictive marker in AML is established guideline consensus.

text: Patients with complex_karyotype=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with complex_karyotype=0.
rationale: Complex karyotype is a textbook adverse-risk feature in AML with well-known poor response.

rationale: FLT3-ITD as an adverse marker is established AML guideline knowledge with dedicated targeted therapies.

text: Patients with idh2_mutation=1 have a higher objective_response rate than patients with idh2_mutation=0.
rationale: IDH2 mutations are established AML markers with FDA-approved enasidenib and known response associations.

text: Patients with secondary_aml=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with secondary_aml=0.
rationale: Secondary AML having worse response than de novo AML is standard guideline/textbook knowledge.

text: Patients with unfit_for_intensive=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with unfit_for_intensive=0.
rationale: Unfit-for-intensive-therapy patients having lower response rates is a defining clinical paradigm in AML treatment selection.

text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=1 is associated with higher objective_response than treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=0.
rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine improving response in AML (especially unfit patients) is established guideline-level evidence from VIALE-A.

text: treatment_7plus3=1 is associated with higher objective_response than treatment_7plus3=0.
rationale: 7+3 induction achieving higher response than non-intensive regimens in fit AML patients is standard guideline knowledge.

text: treatment_midostaurin=1 is associated with higher objective_response than treatment_midostaurin=0.
rationale: Midostaurin's benefit in FLT3-mutated AML is established and reflected in guidelines.

text: treatment_gilteritinib=1 is associated with higher objective_response than treatment_gilteritinib=0.
rationale: Gilteritinib's activity in relapsed/refractory FLT3-mutated AML is a guideline-endorsed standard.

text: treatment_ivosidenib=1 is associated with higher objective_response than treatment_ivosidenib=0.
rationale: Ivosidenib's activity in IDH1-mutant AML is a well-established, guideline-recommended indication.

text: treatment_enasidenib=1 is associated with higher objective_response than treatment_enasidenib=0.
rationale: Enasidenib's activity in IDH2-mutant AML is a well-established, guideline-recommended indication.

text: Among patients with flt3_itd=1, treatment_midostaurin=1 produces a larger increase in objective_response than the same treatment among flt3_itd=0 patients (positive treatment_midostaurin x flt3_itd interaction on the log-odds scale).
rationale: The FLT3-ITD-specific benefit of midostaurin is the canonical biomarker-matched indication from RATIFY.

text: Among patients with flt3_itd=1, treatment_gilteritinib=1 produces a larger increase in objective_response than the same treatment among flt3_itd=0 patients (positive treatment_gilteritinib x flt3_itd interaction).
rationale: Gilteritinib's preferential efficacy in FLT3-ITD AML is the established biomarker-matched indication (ADMIRAL).

text: Among patients with flt3_tkd=1, treatment_midostaurin=1 produces a larger increase in objective_response than among flt3_tkd=0 patients (positive interaction).
rationale: Midostaurin's label and guidelines explicitly include FLT3-TKD mutations as a matched indication.

text: Among patients with flt3_tkd=1, treatment_gilteritinib=1 produces a larger increase in objective_response than among flt3_tkd=0 patients (positive interaction).
rationale: Gilteritinib is approved/recommended for FLT3-TKD as well as ITD mutations, a guideline-level association.

text: Among patients with idh1_mutation=1, treatment_ivosidenib=1 produces a larger increase in objective_response than among idh1_mutation=0 patients (positive treatment_ivosidenib x idh1_mutation interaction). This is the canonical biomarker-matched indication.
rationale: This is explicitly the canonical IDH1-matched indication for ivosidenib.

text: Among patients with idh2_mutation=1, treatment_enasidenib=1 produces a larger increase in objective_response than among idh2_mutation=0 patients (positive treatment_enasidenib x idh2_mutation interaction). Canonical biomarker-matched indication.
rationale: This is explicitly the canonical IDH2-matched indication for enasidenib.

text: Off-target check: treatment_ivosidenib does NOT produce a larger increase in objective_response among idh2_mutation=1 vs idh2_mutation=0 patients (no interaction expected).
rationale: Ivosidenib targets IDH1, not IDH2; lack of IDH2-mutation interaction is the expected on-label paradigm and would appear in any guideline.

text: Off-target check: treatment_enasidenib does NOT produce a larger increase in objective_response among idh1_mutation=1 vs idh1_mutation=0 patients.
rationale: Enasidenib targets IDH2, not IDH1; absence of an IDH1-mutation interaction is standard pharmacology, not a novel claim.

text: Among patients with unfit_for_intensive=1, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=1 yields a larger increase in objective_response than among unfit_for_intensive=0 patients (positive ven/aza x unfit interaction; ven/aza is the standard for unfit patients).
rationale: Venetoclax/azacitidine is the guideline-endorsed standard for unfit AML patients; greater benefit in this subgroup is established consensus.

text: Among patients with unfit_for_intensive=1, treatment_7plus3=1 yields a SMALLER increase (or harm) in objective_response than among fit patients (negative 7+3 x unfit interaction; intensive chemo is poorly tolerated in unfit patients).
rationale: Intensive 7+3 chemotherapy is well known to be poorly tolerated and less effective in unfit patients per all AML guidelines.

text: The benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response is larger in older patients (age>=75) than in patients <60.
rationale: Ven/aza was specifically developed and approved for older/unfit patients; larger benefit in age>=75 reflects guideline indication, not a novel finding.

text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response (negative coefficient on ecog_ps in adjusted logistic regression).
rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse response is a textbook prognostic association across oncology.

text: Older age_years is associated with lower objective_response, after adjusting for ECOG and disease biology.
rationale: Older age predicting lower response in AML, even after adjustment, is a long-established prognostic factor in standard guidelines.

text: After adjusting for age, ECOG, secondary AML, TP53, and complex karyotype, npm1_mutation remains a positive predictor of objective_response.
rationale: Claiming NPM1 retains independent positive predictive value after adjusting for TP53, complex karyotype, secondary AML, age, and ECOG is a multivariable-conditional claim beyond standard ELN risk-group statements.

text: Self-identified Black patients have a lower objective_response rate than white patients.
rationale: Race-based disparity in objective response (not just survival/access) within a specific AML treatment dataset is not a guideline-level established association.

text: Hispanic patients have a lower objective_response rate than white patients.
rationale: A Hispanic-vs-white objective response gap in AML is not part of standard guideline/textbook consensus and would constitute a novel disparities finding.

text: Asian patients have a different objective_response rate than white patients.
rationale: Racial disparities in cancer outcomes are well-documented in guidelines and oncology literature.

text: Uninsured patients have a lower objective_response rate than privately insured patients.
rationale: Insurance status as a predictor of cancer outcomes is a well-established disparity finding.

text: Patients with rural_residence=1 have a lower objective_response rate than urban patients.
rationale: Rural-urban disparities in cancer outcomes are widely recognized in oncology epidemiology.

text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response (positive coefficient).
rationale: Albumin as a prognostic marker reflecting nutritional/inflammatory status is standard textbook knowledge.

text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response.
rationale: Elevated CRP as an adverse prognostic inflammatory marker is well-established across cancers.

text: Higher blast_pct_marrow is associated with lower objective_response.
rationale: Higher marrow blast percentage indicating worse outcomes is a foundational hematologic oncology principle.

text: Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.
rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss as an adverse prognostic factor is textbook oncology knowledge.

text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.
rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker is well-established and incorporated into multiple staging systems.

text: Higher neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (nlr) is associated with lower objective_response.
rationale: Elevated NLR as an adverse prognostic marker is widely established across many cancer types.

text: Higher wbc_k_per_ul (presenting WBC) is associated with lower objective_response.
rationale: Elevated presenting WBC as an adverse prognostic factor (especially in leukemias) is standard guideline knowledge.

text: At least one of the 24 candidate SNPs (snp_rs*) shows an association with objective_response that survives Bonferroni correction (p<0.05/24=0.00208).
rationale: Screening 24 candidate SNPs for an association with response is a generic statistical exercise, not a disease-specific novel claim.

text: snp_rs1050828 is positively associated with objective_response (the most significant single SNP in screening).
rationale: Identifying the top SNP from a screen as positively associated is a tautological screening result, not a guideline-level novel insight.

text: In a multivariable logistic model that simultaneously adjusts for ECOG, age, key labs (albumin, CRP, blasts, weight loss), AML mutations, treatments, and race, ecog_ps remains the dominant negative predictor of objective_response.
rationale: ECOG performance status being the dominant negative predictor of response is a well-established oncology paradigm reflected in every AML guideline.

text: In the same multivariable model, idh1_mutation remains a strong positive predictor of objective_response, independent of treatment and demographic factors.
rationale: IDH1 mutation as a favorable predictor of response, independent of treatment, is a standard known association reflected in current AML guidelines and practice.

text: After multivariable adjustment, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine retains a positive coefficient on objective_response.
rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine improving response in AML is a guideline-endorsed standard-of-care finding, not novel.

text: After multivariable adjustment for clinical and biological covariates, Black race retains a negative association with objective_response (i.e., the disparity is not fully explained by measured covariates).
rationale: A residual race-based disparity in response after adjusting for clinical and biological covariates is a speculative finding beyond guideline consensus.

text: The benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response is larger in patients with ECOG 0 (fitter) than in those with ECOG 2 (sicker), in absolute response-rate terms.
rationale: An ECOG-conditional differential absolute benefit of venetoclax-azacitidine (larger in ECOG 0 than ECOG 2) is a subgroup-specific interaction claim not codified in guidelines.

text: Patients with flt3_itd=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with flt3_itd=0; we expect flt3_itd+ to have lower response (poor-prognosis marker).

text: There is a positive multiplicative interaction between idh1_mutation and treatment_ivosidenib on objective_response (added benefit of ivosidenib in IDH1-mutant patients).

text: There is a negative multiplicative interaction between treatment_7plus3 and secondary_aml on objective_response.

text: In a single multivariable logistic model that jointly includes age, sex, ECOG, fitness flag, all major biomarkers, key labs, all treatments, and four key interaction terms (ven_aza*npm1, ven_aza*unfit, 7plus3*complex_karyotype, idh1*ivosidenib), the four-pronged interaction structure replicates the patterns identified earlier: ven_aza*npm1 positive, ven_aza*unfit positive, 7plus3*complex_karyotype negative, and idh1*ivosidenib negative.
rationale: The specific joint multivariable interaction structure (notably ven/aza*NPM1 positive, ven/aza*unfit positive, 7+3*complex karyotype negative, IDH1*ivosidenib negative) as a combined predictive pattern is not a standard guideline claim.

text: After adjusting for the standard clinical/biomarker covariates, the predictive structure of treatment effects in this AML cohort departs from accepted clinical pharmacology: targeted therapies for IDH1, IDH2, and FLT3 do not produce any detectable benefit in their nominally targeted subgroups.
rationale: Claiming that IDH1, IDH2, and FLT3 targeted agents produce no detectable benefit in their targeted subgroups contradicts established pharmacology and would not appear in any guideline.

text: Across the cohort, the strongest determinant of objective_response is performance status (ecog_ps) plus systemic-illness markers (weight_loss_pct_6mo, blast_pct_marrow, albumin_g_dl, crp_mg_l, wbc_k_per_ul), not classical AML risk genetics (TP53, complex karyotype, secondary AML, FLT3-ITD, age).
rationale: Asserting that performance status and systemic-illness labs (CRP, albumin, weight loss) outweigh classical AML risk genetics like TP53 and complex karyotype as response determinants goes beyond standard guideline framing.

text: Higher dyspnea_grade is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).

text: Higher appetite_loss_grade is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).

text: Higher cough_grade is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).

text: PFS differs across insurance_type categories (one-way ANOVA on pfs_months by insurance_type).

text: PFS differs across race_ethnicity categories (one-way ANOVA on pfs_months by race_ethnicity).

text: Postmenopausal patients (postmenopausal=1) have different pfs_months than premenopausal (postmenopausal=0).

text: snp_rs1065852 (CYP2D6*10 proxy) modifies the PFS effect of treatment_tamoxifen (non-zero interaction in OLS).
rationale: CYP2D6 genotype modulating tamoxifen efficacy via endoxifen metabolism is a classic, textbook pharmacogenomic association.

text: snp_rs4244285 (CYP2C19*2 proxy) modifies the PFS effect of treatment_palbociclib (non-zero interaction).
rationale: Palbociclib is metabolized primarily by CYP3A4, so a CYP2C19*2 interaction with palbociclib PFS is not in guidelines and would be a novel pharmacogenomic claim.

text: snp_rs1801133 (MTHFR C677T proxy) is associated with pfs_months on its own (non-zero OLS slope).
rationale: MTHFR C677T is linked to antifolate toxicity but not established as an independent PFS predictor in oncology guidelines.

text: snp_rs429358 (APOE proxy) is associated with pfs_months on its own (non-zero OLS slope).
rationale: APOE genotype is a neurologic/cardiovascular marker and is not a guideline-level oncology PFS predictor.

text: snp_rs1800629 (TNF-alpha promoter proxy) modifies the PFS effect of treatment_pembrolizumab (non-zero interaction).
rationale: A TNF-alpha promoter polymorphism modifying pembrolizumab efficacy is a speculative cross-pathway immunogenetic claim absent from guidelines.

text: Patients with diabetes_mellitus = 1 have shorter pfs_months than non-diabetics.
rationale: Diabetes as a comorbidity associated with worse cancer outcomes is a well-studied and frequently cited association.

text: Patients with chronic_kidney_disease = 1 have shorter pfs_months.

text: Patients with heart_failure = 1 have shorter pfs_months.

text: Patients with interstitial_lung_disease_history = 1 have shorter pfs_months.

text: Patients with prior_chemotherapy = 1 have shorter pfs_months than chemo-naive patients.

text: More prior_lines_of_therapy is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
rationale: More prior lines of therapy correlating with shorter PFS is a well-established prognostic principle.

text: Higher years_since_diagnosis is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
rationale: Longer disease duration generally implying more advanced/progressive disease and shorter PFS is intuitive guideline-level reasoning, though direction can vary.

text: After adjusting for stage_iv, has_brain_mets, ecog_ps, age_years, ldh_u_l, albumin_g_dl, er_positive, her2_positive, and prior_lines_of_therapy, treatment_palbociclib retains a positive, significant association with pfs_months.
rationale: Palbociclib's PFS benefit in HR+/HER2- metastatic breast cancer is established by PALOMA trials and is standard guideline content.

text: After adjustment in the same multivariable OLS, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, ldh_u_l, and prior_lines_of_therapy each retain an independent negative association with pfs_months, while albumin_g_dl and er_positive retain independent positive associations.
rationale: These prognostic directions (ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, LDH, prior lines negative; albumin, ER+ positive) are textbook prognostic associations.

text: The PFS benefit of treatment_palbociclib differs by stage_iv (non-zero interaction term in OLS of pfs_months on treatment_palbociclib * stage_iv adjusted for ecog_ps and age_years).
rationale: A stage_iv-specific modification of palbociclib's PFS effect is a subgroup-conditional interaction not established in guidelines.

text: Within er_positive=1 patients, treatment_palbociclib still extends pfs_months relative to those not on palbo (positive simple effect in stratified t-test).
rationale: Palbociclib benefit within ER+ patients is the canonical guideline-endorsed indication, not novel.

text: Patients with stage_iv=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with stage_iv=0.
rationale: Stage IV disease having shorter PFS than non-stage IV is a fundamental, guideline-level prognostic association.

text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative monotonic relationship across the ordinal scale).

text: treatment_bevacizumab=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than treatment_bevacizumab=0 patients (overall main effect).

text: treatment_pembrolizumab=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab=0 patients (overall main effect).

text: Among RAS-wildtype patients (kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0), treatment_cetuximab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_cetuximab=0; the cetuximab×RAS-status interaction is positive.

text: Among msi_high=1 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab=0; pembrolizumab×MSI interaction is positive.
rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in MSI-high tumors is a foundational guideline-level association.

text: Among braf_v600e=1 patients, treatment_encorafenib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_encorafenib=0; encorafenib×BRAF interaction is positive.
rationale: Encorafenib benefit in BRAF V600E-mutant CRC is established guideline knowledge.

text: Among her2_amplified=1 patients, treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=0; the interaction is positive.

text: The +0.97-month regorafenib main effect on pfs_months persists after multivariable adjustment for prognostic covariates (age, ECOG, stage_iv, biomarkers, labs).
rationale: A regorafenib main-effect PFS benefit persisting after multivariable adjustment in this cohort is a dataset-specific quantitative claim beyond guideline consensus.

text: The regorafenib benefit on pfs_months differs between stage_iv=1 and stage_iv=0 (interaction).
rationale: Stage IV-conditional differential regorafenib effect is a subgroup interaction not addressed in guidelines.

text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation).
rationale: Albumin as a positive prognostic factor for survival/PFS is a well-known textbook association.

text: Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
rationale: Cancer cachexia and weight loss are well-established adverse prognostic factors documented in standard oncology guidelines and textbooks.

text: Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
rationale: Elevated CEA as an adverse prognostic marker is a textbook association in multiple solid tumors.

text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.

text: Older age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation), after adjustment for ECOG and stage.

text: Mean pfs_months differs between sex_female=1 and sex_female=0.

text: Mean pfs_months differs across race_ethnicity categories.

text: Mean pfs_months differs across insurance_type categories (medicaid/medicare/private/uninsured).

text: Mean pfs_months differs between rural_residence=1 and rural_residence=0.
rationale: Rural-urban disparities in cancer outcomes are well-documented in standard references and not a novel claim.

text: Presence of liver_mets, bone_mets, adrenal_mets, pleural_effusion, pericardial_effusion, or contralateral_lung_mets is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Visceral metastases (liver, bone, effusions, etc.) as adverse prognostic factors are textbook associations across solid tumors.

text: Higher symptom-grade scores (fatigue_grade, pain_nrs, dyspnea_grade, cough_grade, appetite_loss_grade) are associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Higher symptom burden correlating with worse PFS is a well-established prognostic association reflected in performance status assessments in standard guidelines.

text: Comorbidities such as diabetes_mellitus, copd, chronic_kidney_disease, heart_failure, atrial_fibrillation, prior_malignancy, depression_anxiety_diagnosis are associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Comorbidity burden as a negative prognostic factor is standard knowledge incorporated into oncology guidelines and risk assessment tools.

text: At least one of the 27 SNP columns shows a Bonferroni-significant association with pfs_months (alpha = 0.05/27 = 1.85e-3).
rationale: Specific SNP associations with PFS in this disease context are not established germline pharmacogenomic predictors in current treatment guidelines.

text: Greater prior_lines_of_therapy is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: More prior lines of therapy predicting shorter PFS in subsequent treatment is well-established in refractory disease guidelines.

text: Prior treatment exposures (chemotherapy, radiation, surgery, immunotherapy, targeted therapy) are associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Heavier prior treatment exposure correlating with worse outcomes is a standard prognostic observation in guidelines.

text: More years_since_diagnosis is associated with longer pfs_months.
rationale: Indolent disease biology reflected by longer time since diagnosis predicting better PFS is a recognized prognostic concept.

text: In a multivariable OLS model of pfs_months including age, sex, ECOG, stage, sidedness, key biomarkers (kras, nras, braf_v600e, msi_high, her2_amplified), labs (cea, albumin, ldh, weight loss, crp, nlr), and all six treatments, the significant prognostic predictors are age (+), albumin (+), regorafenib (+) and ECOG, stage_iv, right_sided_primary, kras_mutation, braf_v600e, cea_ng_ml, ldh_u_l, weight_loss_pct_6mo (-).
rationale: These multivariable prognostic associations (ECOG, stage, albumin, LDH, CEA, KRAS, BRAF, sidedness) are all established prognostic factors in colorectal cancer guidelines.

text: The regorafenib benefit on pfs_months is restricted to kras_mutation=0 patients; kras_mutation=1 patients derive no benefit (negative regorafenib×KRAS interaction).
rationale: A KRAS-wild-type-restricted benefit of regorafenib is not an established guideline biomarker; regorafenib is approved regardless of KRAS status.

text: The regorafenib benefit is absent or attenuated in braf_v600e=1 patients (negative regorafenib×BRAF interaction).
rationale: BRAF V600E-specific attenuation of regorafenib benefit is not an established predictive biomarker in current guidelines.

text: The regorafenib benefit is restricted to left-sided primaries (right_sided_primary=0); right-sided patients derive no benefit (negative regorafenib×right_sided interaction).
rationale: Sidedness as a predictive biomarker for regorafenib (rather than for anti-EGFR therapy) is not established in treatment guidelines.

text: The regorafenib benefit is similar in magnitude in msi_high=1 and msi_high=0 patients (no regorafenib×MSI interaction).
rationale: Regorafenib is used in refractory mCRC regardless of MSI status; absence of MSI interaction aligns with standard practice and would not be a novel claim.

text: The regorafenib benefit is similar across ecog_ps strata (0,1,2).
rationale: Guidelines restrict regorafenib largely to ECOG 0-1 due to toxicity, so claiming equivalent benefit across ECOG 0/1/2 goes beyond guideline consensus.

text: Within the joint subgroup kras_mutation=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 (left-sided RAS-WT), the regorafenib benefit on pfs_months exceeds 2 months and is the largest of any 2x2 KRAS×sidedness cell.
rationale: Sidedness and RAS status are canonical predictors for anti-EGFR therapy, not regorafenib; claiming the largest regorafenib PFS benefit in left-sided RAS-WT is a non-guideline cross-pathway claim.

text: Even in the canonical indication subgroup (kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND right_sided_primary=0), treatment_cetuximab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_cetuximab=0.
rationale: Cetuximab benefit in RAS/RAF-wildtype left-sided mCRC is the textbook indication and appears in every current guideline.

text: Within braf_v600e=1, the combination treatment_encorafenib=1 AND treatment_cetuximab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than other regimens.
rationale: Encorafenib+cetuximab for BRAF V600E mCRC is the established BEACON regimen and standard guideline recommendation.

text: After all probing, the dominant signals for pfs_months are: (1) older age (+), albumin (+), and treatment_regorafenib (+); (2) higher ECOG-PS (-), stage_iv (-), right_sided_primary (-), kras_mutation (-), braf_v600e (-), CEA (-), LDH (-), weight loss (-); (3) the regorafenib benefit is concentrated in KRAS-wildtype, BRAF-wildtype, left-sided patients; (4) cetuximab/pembrolizumab/encorafenib/trastuzumab+tucatinib show NO biomarker-targeted PFS benefit; (5) demographics, SNPs, comorbidities, mets sites and symptoms are not prognostic in this cohort.
rationale: The composite contains non-guideline claims (older age as positive PFS predictor, regorafenib benefit concentrated in KRAS/BRAF-WT left-sided patients, and absence of biomarker-targeted benefit for cetuximab/encorafenib/trastuzumab+tucatinib) that contradict or extend beyond established consensus.

text: Patients receiving treatment_regorafenib have longer pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_regorafenib (positive main effect on PFS).

text: Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib have longer pfs_months than patients not receiving it (positive main effect on PFS).
