text: There is a positive interaction between brca2_mutation and treatment_olaparib on pfs_months: BRCA2-mutated patients receiving olaparib have substantially longer PFS than expected from main effects alone.
rationale: BRCA2-mutated prostate cancer responding preferentially to olaparib is the basis of its guideline-endorsed indication.

text: There is a positive interaction between msi_high and treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months: MSI-high patients benefit from pembrolizumab more than MSI-low patients.
rationale: MSI-high tumors deriving greater benefit from pembrolizumab is a tumor-agnostic guideline-level principle.

text: There is a negative interaction between ar_v7_positive and treatment_enzalutamide on pfs_months: AR-V7+ patients derive less benefit (or harm) from enzalutamide than AR-V7- patients.
rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting resistance to enzalutamide is a well-established association in mCRPC guidelines and textbooks.

text: There is a negative interaction between ar_v7_positive and treatment_abiraterone on pfs_months: AR-V7+ patients derive less benefit from abiraterone than AR-V7- patients.
rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting resistance to abiraterone is established consensus from Antonarakis et al. and standard prostate cancer references.

text: There is a positive interaction between psma_high and treatment_lu177_psma on pfs_months: PSMA-high tumors respond better to PSMA-targeted radioligand therapy.
rationale: PSMA-high expression predicting response to Lu-177-PSMA is the established VISION/TheraP paradigm and is in current guidelines.

text: Higher age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (unexpected positive correlation in this dataset).
rationale: A positive correlation between older age and longer PFS contradicts the standard expectation that older/frailer patients fare worse.

text: After multivariable adjustment for age, ECOG, mCRPC, visceral_mets, PSA, albumin, LDH, the BRCA2 x olaparib interaction term remains a strong positive predictor of pfs_months.
rationale: BRCA2-mutant prostate cancer benefiting from olaparib is the established PROfound-trial paradigm reflected in guidelines.

text: After multivariable adjustment, age_years remains positively associated with pfs_months.
rationale: Persistence of a positive ageâ€“PFS association after multivariable adjustment is counter to standard prognostic teaching.

text: After multivariable adjustment, none of the targeted-therapy main effects (enzalutamide, abiraterone, docetaxel, lu177_psma, pembrolizumab) is materially associated with pfs_months.
rationale: A claim that none of the major active prostate cancer therapies shows a main effect on PFS contradicts established trial-based efficacy consensus.

text: There is no clinically meaningful interaction between mcrpc and any treatment on pfs_months.
rationale: Absence of any treatment-by-mCRPC-state interaction is non-trivial because guidelines distinguish hormone-sensitive vs castration-resistant treatment effects.

text: None of the listed comorbidities (diabetes_mellitus, hypertension, copd, chronic_kidney_disease, heart_failure, coronary_artery_disease, atrial_fibrillation, venous_thromboembolism_history, autoimmune_disease, hepatitis_b/c_history, hiv_positive, prior_malignancy, depression_anxiety_diagnosis, interstitial_lung_disease_history) is associated with pfs_months.
rationale: Most of these comorbidities are not established independent predictors of prostate cancer PFS, so a null finding aligns with conventional expectations.

text: Among the routine lab values, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l and total_bilirubin_mg_dl are inversely associated with pfs_months (markers of liver/bone disease burden).
rationale: Elevated alkaline phosphatase and bilirubin reflecting bone/liver burden and predicting worse PFS is a standard prognostic association in mCRPC.

text: Symptom grades (fatigue_grade, pain_nrs, dyspnea_grade, cough_grade, appetite_loss_grade) are inversely associated with pfs_months.
rationale: Symptom burden inversely correlating with PFS is a well-established prognostic finding documented in oncology guidelines and PRO literature.

text: Prior therapies (prior_chemotherapy, prior_radiation, prior_surgery, prior_immunotherapy, prior_targeted_therapy) and prior_lines_of_therapy are inversely associated with pfs_months.
rationale: Heavier prior treatment exposure and more prior lines predicting shorter PFS is standard prognostic knowledge reflected in treatment guidelines.

text: Mean pfs_months differs across race_ethnicity categories.
rationale: Disparities in cancer outcomes by race/ethnicity are widely recognized in guideline and population health literature.

text: Mean pfs_months differs across insurance_type categories (uninsured shorter than private/medicare).
rationale: Worse cancer outcomes among uninsured patients compared with insured groups is a well-documented health disparity, not a novel claim.

text: Patients with rural_residence=1 have shorter pfs_months than urban residents.
rationale: Rural residence as a predictor of worse oncology outcomes is an established disparities finding in the literature.

text: Among the 25 SNP variables, none shows a real association with pfs_months beyond what is expected by chance.
rationale: A null result for an exploratory SNP panel is the expected default and does not constitute a novel mechanistic claim.

text: Restricting analysis to mcrpc=1, the AR-V7 x enzalutamide interaction predicts pfs_months (negative interaction).
rationale: AR-V7 expression predicting resistance to enzalutamide in mCRPC is established paradigm consensus and appears in prostate cancer guidelines.

text: Restricting analysis to mcrpc=1, the AR-V7 x abiraterone interaction predicts pfs_months (negative interaction).
rationale: AR-V7 predicting resistance to abiraterone in mCRPC is a well-known association documented in standard prostate cancer references.

text: Restricting analysis to mcrpc=1, the PSMA-high x lu177-PSMA interaction predicts pfs_months (positive interaction).
rationale: PSMA-high expression predicting benefit from Lu-177-PSMA therapy is established (VISION trial) and codified in current mCRPC guidelines.

text: Restricting analysis to mcrpc=1, the BRCA2 x olaparib interaction remains strongly positive.
rationale: BRCA2 alterations predicting response to olaparib in mCRPC is established paradigm consensus reflected in FDA labeling and guidelines.

text: The age x pfs_months relationship holds within every ECOG stratum (not confounded by performance status).
rationale: Claiming the age-PFS relationship is invariant across ECOG strata is a non-guideline subgroup-conditional assertion.

text: ECOG x treatment_olaparib interaction predicts pfs_months (poor PS dampens benefit).
rationale: An ECOG-by-olaparib interaction modifying PFS benefit is not a standard guideline claim and goes beyond the established BRCA-restricted indication.

text: Albumin x treatment_olaparib interaction predicts pfs_months.
rationale: An albumin-by-olaparib interaction is a speculative biomarker-modifier claim not found in guidelines.

text: Number of concomitant treatments (n_tx) is associated with pfs_months.
rationale: Linking number of concomitant medications to PFS is a non-standard pharmacologic/host-context claim absent from guidelines.

text: Among non-prostate-canonical gene alterations (her2_amplification, met_exon14_skipping, ret_fusion, ros1_fusion, braf_v600e, ntrk_fusion, nrg1_fusion, fgfr_alteration, cdkn2a_loss, tp53_mutation, keap1_mutation, pik3ca_mutation, pten_loss), none is independently associated with pfs_months.
rationale: A blanket null claim that a panel of cross-pathway alterations has no prognostic effect in prostate cancer is a speculative cross-pathway assertion not in guidelines.

text: Sex_female=1 patients differ from sex_female=0 patients in pfs_months.
rationale: Prostate cancer is overwhelmingly male, so a sex difference claim is either trivially confounded or not a guideline-level oncology insight; this is a basic demographic comparison, not a novel mechanistic hypothesis.

text: The BRCA2 x olaparib effect on pfs_months is similar across all three ECOG strata (i.e., does not require performance-status restriction).
rationale: Asserting BRCA2-olaparib benefit is uniform across ECOG strata is a subgroup-invariance claim beyond guideline consensus, which doesn't specify PS-independence.

text: The BRCA2 x olaparib effect is larger in older patients (because PFS itself is longer at older ages, the absolute benefit may scale).
rationale: An age-scaled magnification of BRCA2-olaparib benefit is a speculative effect-modification hypothesis not in guidelines.

text: BRCA2_mutation has no independent prognostic effect on pfs_months among patients NOT receiving olaparib.
rationale: BRCA2's prognostic role independent of PARP inhibition is well-discussed in prostate cancer literature/guidelines as part of HRR biology.

text: Olaparib has no independent treatment effect on pfs_months among BRCA2-NEGATIVE patients.
rationale: Olaparib's lack of benefit in BRCA-negative prostate cancer is established guideline knowledge underpinning the biomarker-restricted indication.

text: Visceral_mets is more strongly associated with shorter pfs_months in non-mCRPC patients than in mCRPC patients.
rationale: Claim that visceral metastases impact PFS more strongly in non-mCRPC than mCRPC is a subgroup-conditional effect-modification claim not in standard guidelines.

text: In a comprehensive multivariable OLS model of pfs_months including age, ECOG, mCRPC, visceral_mets, PSA, albumin, LDH, BRCA2, treatment_olaparib, BRCA2 x olaparib interaction, AR-V7, MSI-high, PSMA-high, and the other five treatments, the dominant predictors are age (positive), ECOG (negative), mCRPC (negative), albumin (positive), LDH (negative), PSA (negative), and the BRCA2 x olaparib interaction (positive).
rationale: Identifying age, ECOG, albumin, LDH, PSA, mCRPC status, and BRCA2-olaparib interaction as dominant PFS predictors restates well-established prognostic and predictive factors.

text: After full multivariable adjustment, treatments enzalutamide, abiraterone, docetaxel, lu177-psma, pembrolizumab show no individual main effect on pfs_months.
rationale: Null main effects of standard prostate cancer therapies after adjustment in a mixed cohort is an unsurprising statistical observation, not a guideline-relevant claim.

text: After multivariable adjustment, ar_v7_positive itself is associated with slightly shorter pfs_months (residual prognostic effect, not modified by treatment).
rationale: A residual independent prognostic effect of AR-V7 positivity after full adjustment, not modified by treatment, goes beyond the standard guideline framing of AR-V7 as a predictor of AR-targeted therapy resistance.

text: After multivariable adjustment, psma_high is associated with slightly longer pfs_months as a prognostic marker (independent of Lu177 use).
rationale: PSMA-high as an independent favorable prognostic marker for PFS independent of Lu177-PSMA use is not part of standard oncology consensus, where PSMA expression is framed as a theranostic predictor.

text: Across all hypotheses tested, the only large, replicable, treatment-relevant signal is the BRCA2_mutation x treatment_olaparib interaction increasing pfs_months by ~1.5 months.
rationale: BRCA2 x olaparib interaction improving PFS in prostate cancer is established guideline knowledge (PROfound trial).

text: The strongest prognostic signals for pfs_months are age_years (positive), ECOG_PS (negative), mCRPC (negative), albumin_g_dl (positive), and LDH_u_l (negative); together these explain >90% of PFS variance.
rationale: Age, ECOG, mCRPC status, albumin, and LDH as dominant prognostic factors for PFS in prostate cancer are textbook prognostic variables.

text: Several biomarker-treatment pairings biologically expected to show interaction (msi_high x pembrolizumab, ar_v7_positive x enzalutamide/abiraterone, psma_high x lu177-psma) show no detectable effect-modification on pfs_months in this cohort.
rationale: A negative finding that MSI-high x pembrolizumab, AR-V7 x AR-targeted therapy, and PSMA-high x Lu177-PSMA show no effect modification contradicts established expectations and would be a noteworthy departure from guideline-endorsed predictive biomarkers.

text: Race_ethnicity, insurance_type, rural_residence, education_years, and the 25 SNP variables show no clinically meaningful association with pfs_months.
rationale: Null associations of sociodemographic variables and unselected SNPs with PFS is an expected negative finding consistent with standard expectations.

text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have longer mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib (positive main effect).
rationale: Olaparib benefit in HRR-mutant mCRPC is established and guideline-endorsed.

text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have longer mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab (positive main effect).
rationale: Pembrolizumab use in MSI-H/dMMR or TMB-high tumors is standard guideline knowledge.

text: Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel have shorter mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_docetaxel (negative main effect, reflecting selection of docetaxel for higher-risk disease).
rationale: Docetaxel being given to higher-risk patients producing shorter PFS reflects well-known confounding by indication.

text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide have a different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_enzalutamide.
rationale: Enzalutamide effect on PFS in prostate cancer is a guideline-level standard association.

text: Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone have a different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_abiraterone.
rationale: Abiraterone effect on PFS in prostate cancer is established standard-of-care knowledge.

text: Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma have a different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_lu177_psma.
rationale: Lu-177-PSMA improving PFS in PSMA-positive mCRPC is established from VISION and is guideline-endorsed.

text: Higher age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (older patients have worse prognosis).
rationale: Older age associating with worse prognosis is textbook prognostic information.

text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse outcomes is a textbook prognostic factor.

text: Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Higher PSA correlating with worse prostate cancer outcomes is a standard prognostic association.

text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
rationale: Low albumin as a poor prognostic marker is well-established across oncology.

text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Weight loss is a well-established adverse prognostic factor in advanced cancer and standard cachexia/performance literature.

text: mcrpc=1 (castration-resistant disease) is associated with shorter pfs_months than mcrpc=0.
rationale: Castration resistance is a defining adverse prognostic state in prostate cancer and universally recognized in guidelines.

text: treatment_olaparib improves pfs_months specifically in patients with brca2_mutation=1 (positive treatment-by-biomarker interaction).
rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA2-mutated prostate cancer is established (PROfound trial) and appears in current treatment guidelines.

text: treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months specifically in patients with msi_high=1 (positive treatment-by-biomarker interaction).
rationale: Pembrolizumab efficacy in MSI-high tumors is FDA-approved tissue-agnostic indication and standard guideline knowledge.

text: treatment_lu177_psma improves pfs_months specifically in patients with psma_high=1 (positive treatment-by-biomarker interaction).
rationale: Lu-177-PSMA benefit in PSMA-high mCRPC is established by the VISION trial and incorporated into current guidelines.

text: After adjusting for prognostic covariates (age_years, ecog_ps, psa_ng_ml, albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, mcrpc), treatment_olaparib has a significant positive interaction with brca2_mutation on pfs_months, while its main effect in brca2_mutation=0 patients is null.
rationale: This restates the established BRCA2-conditional olaparib benefit with covariate adjustment, consistent with guideline-level knowledge.

text: In a multivariable OLS model, the marginal main effects of treatment_enzalutamide, treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, and treatment_lu177_psma on pfs_months are not significantly different from zero (treatments roughly balanced across prognostic risk).
rationale: Claiming null marginal effects for enzalutamide, abiraterone, and docetaxel contradicts guideline-established survival benefits and is a dataset-specific, non-standard claim.

text: After adjusting for prognostic covariates, brca2_mutation has no main effect on pfs_months independent of olaparib treatment (its prognostic value is conditional on therapy).
rationale: Asserting BRCA2 has no independent prognostic effect outside olaparib context is a nuanced, treatment-conditional claim not established in guidelines.

text: In BRCA2-mutated patients (brca2_mutation=1), treatment_olaparib increases pfs_months by approximately 1.4 months after adjustment for prognostic covariates.
rationale: A specific quantitative effect size (~1.4 months PFS) in a covariate-adjusted subgroup is a dataset-specific finding beyond guideline consensus.

text: In BRCA2-wildtype patients (brca2_mutation=0), treatment_olaparib has no significant effect on pfs_months after adjustment (i.e., no off-target benefit, and no off-target harm).
rationale: Explicitly asserting no off-target benefit AND no off-target harm of olaparib in BRCA2-wildtype patients after adjustment is a finer-grained claim than guideline statements about biomarker selection.

text: In MSI-high patients, treatment_pembrolizumab does not significantly change pfs_months after adjustment for prognostic covariates.
rationale: A null effect of pembrolizumab in MSI-high patients contradicts established guideline consensus that MSI-high tumors derive significant benefit from immune checkpoint blockade.

text: treatment_enzalutamide has a smaller (less positive or more negative) effect on pfs_months in ar_v7_positive=1 patients than in ar_v7_positive=0 patients (negative interaction).
rationale: AR-V7 splice variant conferring resistance to enzalutamide is a well-established biomarker association documented in guidelines and the Antonarakis NEJM literature.

text: treatment_abiraterone has a smaller effect on pfs_months in ar_v7_positive=1 patients than in ar_v7_positive=0 patients (negative interaction).
rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting reduced response to abiraterone is an established AR-targeted therapy resistance mechanism in standard prostate cancer literature.

text: treatment_docetaxel has a more positive effect on pfs_months in patients with visceral_mets=1 than visceral_mets=0 (chemotherapy preferred for high-burden visceral disease).
rationale: Preferential use of docetaxel for high-volume/visceral metastatic disease is a guideline-level treatment paradigm (e.g., CHAARTED-era prostate cancer recommendations).

text: Mean pfs_months differs across race_ethnicity categories (white, black, hispanic, asian, other).
rationale: Race/ethnicity disparities in cancer outcomes including PFS are widely documented in oncology epidemiology and disparities literature.

text: Mean pfs_months differs across insurance_type categories (medicare, private, medicaid, uninsured).
rationale: Insurance-based disparities in cancer outcomes are an established sociodemographic finding routinely reported in health services research.

text: rural_residence=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than rural_residence=0 patients.
rationale: Rural residence association with worse cancer outcomes is a well-documented disparity in access-to-care and oncology outcomes literature.

text: The brca2_mutation x treatment_olaparib PFS benefit is consistent across race_ethnicity strata (no race-by-interaction effect).
rationale: A race-stratified interaction analysis of BRCA2 x olaparib benefit is a subgroup-conditional pharmacogenomic claim not addressed in current PARP inhibitor guidelines.

text: At least one of the 28 SNPs (snp_rs* columns) has a Pearson correlation with pfs_months that survives Bonferroni correction (alpha=0.05/28=0.0018).
rationale: A Bonferroni-significant germline SNPâ€“PFS pharmacogenomic association would be a novel exploratory finding not present in standard treatment guidelines.

text: Higher comorbidity burden (sum of diabetes_mellitus, hypertension, copd, chronic_kidney_disease, heart_failure, coronary_artery_disease, atrial_fibrillation, prior_malignancy) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Higher comorbidity burden predicting worse cancer outcomes is a well-established prognostic relationship reflected throughout oncology and geriatric oncology literature.

text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months after adjustment for other prognostic factors.
rationale: Elevated LDH is a well-established adverse prognostic factor in mCRPC and appears in standard prognostic nomograms and guidelines.

text: treatment_docetaxel has a less positive effect on pfs_months in patients with ecog_ps=2 (poor PS) than in those with ecog_ps=0 (good PS).
rationale: Diminished benefit and greater toxicity of docetaxel in poor performance status (ECOG 2) patients is standard guideline knowledge.

text: Treatment effects on pfs_months differ by mcrpc status (treatment_olaparib remains effective in both, while AR-targeted treatments have differential effects).
rationale: A claim that olaparib's PFS benefit is preserved across mCRPC vs non-mCRPC states while AR-targeted agents show differential effects by mCRPC status is a speculative subgroup-conditional assertion not in guidelines.

text: age_years has a different relationship to treatment_abiraterone effectiveness than to treatment_enzalutamide effectiveness (age x abi interaction).
rationale: A differential age-by-drug interaction distinguishing abiraterone from enzalutamide effectiveness is not an established guideline finding.

text: In a comprehensive multivariable OLS model that simultaneously includes all six treatment indicators, four key biomarkers (brca2_mutation, ar_v7_positive, msi_high, psma_high), three corresponding treatment-by-biomarker interactions (olaparib:brca2, pembrolizumab:msi_high, lu177:psma), and clinical prognostic factors (age, ecog_ps, psa, albumin, weight_loss, mcrpc, visceral_mets, hemoglobin, alkaline_phosphatase, ldh), the only treatment-by-biomarker interaction that is statistically significant is treatment_olaparib x brca2_mutation, with magnitude approximately +1.4 months PFS.
rationale: The specific multivariable claim that only the olaparib x BRCA2 interaction (â‰ˆ+1.4 months PFS) reaches significance among three biomarker-treatment interactions is a precise quantitative cross-pathway result beyond textbook consensus.

text: In the comprehensive model, age_years remains the strongest single predictor of pfs_months (positive direction in this synthetic cohort), followed by ecog_ps (negative).
rationale: Age being the single strongest predictor of PFS in a positive direction contradicts standard oncology paradigm where older age and worse PS are typically adverse, making this a non-guideline claim.

text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
rationale: ECOG performance status as a prognostic factor for shorter PFS is a well-established standard finding in oncology guidelines.

text: Patients with mcrpc=1 (castration-resistant) have shorter mean pfs_months than those with mcrpc=0.
rationale: Castration-resistant prostate cancer having worse PFS than hormone-sensitive disease is a foundational concept in prostate cancer guidelines.

text: Patients with visceral_mets=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than those without visceral metastases.
rationale: Visceral metastases as an adverse prognostic factor for shorter PFS is a standard risk stratifier in prostate cancer guidelines.

text: Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
rationale: Higher PSA correlating with worse outcomes is a textbook prognostic association in prostate cancer.

text: Higher gleason_score is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
rationale: Higher Gleason score predicting worse PFS is a fundamental, guideline-established prognostic factor.

text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation).
rationale: Albumin as a positive prognostic biomarker is well-established in prostate cancer prognostic models (e.g., Halabi nomogram).

text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic factor is a standard component of validated prostate cancer risk models.

text: Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation).
rationale: Hemoglobin as a positive prognostic factor is included in standard mCRPC prognostic nomograms.

text: Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
rationale: Elevated alkaline phosphatase as an adverse prognostic marker is a well-established finding, particularly reflecting bone disease burden.

text: Patients with bone_mets=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than those without bone metastases.
rationale: Bone metastases as an adverse prognostic factor for shorter PFS is a standard, guideline-recognized association in prostate cancer.

text: Patients with liver_mets=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than those without liver metastases.
rationale: Liver metastases are a well-established adverse prognostic factor in metastatic prostate cancer and appear in guideline risk stratification.

text: Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
rationale: Older age as a negative prognostic factor is standard clinical knowledge and reflected in guideline risk assessments.

text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide=1 have longer mean pfs_months than those not receiving enzalutamide.
rationale: Enzalutamide is a guideline-endorsed standard-of-care that prolongs PFS in metastatic prostate cancer.

text: Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone=1 have longer mean pfs_months than those not receiving abiraterone.
rationale: Abiraterone is a guideline-endorsed standard-of-care that prolongs PFS in metastatic prostate cancer.

text: Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than those not receiving docetaxel (cytotoxic treatment is reserved for sicker patients, confounding-by-indication likely).
rationale: Confounding-by-indication for docetaxel in sicker patients is a well-known clinical phenomenon, not a novel insight.

text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib=1 have longer mean pfs_months than those not receiving olaparib (driven by BRCA2-positive enrichment).
rationale: Olaparib's benefit driven by BRCA2-positive enrichment is the established PROfound-trial paradigm in guidelines.

text: Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma=1 have longer mean pfs_months than those not receiving Lu177-PSMA.
rationale: Lu177-PSMA prolonging PFS in PSMA-positive metastatic prostate cancer is established (VISION trial) and in current guidelines.

text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab=1 have longer mean pfs_months than those not receiving pembrolizumab.
rationale: Pembrolizumab is not a guideline-standard therapy for unselected metastatic prostate cancer and a broad PFS benefit would be unexpected.

text: In patients with brca2_mutation=1, treatment_olaparib is associated with longer pfs_months than no olaparib (predictive biomarker).
rationale: BRCA2 as a predictive biomarker for olaparib response is the canonical PROfound-derived guideline indication.

text: In patients with brca2_mutation=0, treatment_olaparib has little or no effect on pfs_months.
rationale: Lack of olaparib benefit in BRCA2-wildtype patients is the established mirror of the predictive biomarker paradigm in guidelines.

text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_olaparib and brca2_mutation on pfs_months (effect of olaparib is larger in BRCA2-positive patients).
rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA-mutated prostate cancer is established and guideline-endorsed (PROfound).

text: In patients with psma_high=1, treatment_lu177_psma is associated with longer pfs_months than no Lu177-PSMA.
rationale: Lu-177-PSMA efficacy in PSMA-high mCRPC is established and FDA-approved based on VISION.

text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_lu177_psma and psma_high on pfs_months.
rationale: Greater Lu-177-PSMA benefit with higher PSMA expression is an established guideline-level concept underlying patient selection.

text: In patients with msi_high=1, treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with longer pfs_months than no pembrolizumab.
rationale: Pembrolizumab activity in MSI-high tumors is a well-established tissue-agnostic indication.

text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_pembrolizumab and msi_high on pfs_months.
rationale: MSI-high status as a predictor of pembrolizumab benefit is standard guideline knowledge.

text: In patients with ar_v7_positive=1, treatment_enzalutamide is associated with shorter pfs_months relative to AR-V7-negative patients (AR-V7 confers resistance).
rationale: AR-V7 as a marker of resistance to enzalutamide is well-established in prostate cancer literature and practice.

text: There is a negative interaction between treatment_enzalutamide and ar_v7_positive on pfs_months (resistance).
rationale: Negative interaction between enzalutamide and AR-V7 (resistance) is established consensus.

text: There is a negative interaction between treatment_abiraterone and ar_v7_positive on pfs_months (resistance).
rationale: AR-V7-mediated resistance to abiraterone is established consensus from prior AR-V7 biomarker studies.

text: Mean pfs_months declines monotonically across ECOG performance-status groups (ECOG 0 > 1 > 2).
rationale: Worse outcomes with higher ECOG performance status is a universal, textbook prognostic finding.

text: Within mcrpc=1 patients, treatment_enzalutamide is associated with longer pfs_months than no enzalutamide.
rationale: Enzalutamide improves PFS in mCRPC is established standard-of-care knowledge (PREVAIL/AFFIRM).

text: Within mcrpc=0 (HSPC) patients, treatment_enzalutamide is associated with longer pfs_months than no enzalutamide.
rationale: Enzalutamide benefit in hormone-sensitive prostate cancer (HSPC) is established by ENZAMET/ARCHES and is standard guideline care.

text: Within mcrpc=0 (HSPC) patients, treatment_docetaxel is associated with longer pfs_months than no docetaxel (e.g. CHAARTED-style benefit).
rationale: Docetaxel benefit in HSPC (CHAARTED/STAMPEDE) is a well-established guideline-endorsed standard.

text: Within mcrpc=1 patients, treatment_lu177_psma is associated with longer pfs_months than no Lu177-PSMA.
rationale: Lu177-PSMA (VISION trial) prolonging PFS in mCRPC is FDA-approved and guideline-recommended.

text: Higher nlr (neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
rationale: Elevated NLR as a poor prognostic marker is a well-known pan-cancer association reported widely in prostate cancer literature.

text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
rationale: Elevated CRP as a negative prognostic factor in advanced prostate cancer is an established association.

text: Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss as a negative prognostic factor is textbook oncology knowledge.

text: Higher pain_nrs is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
rationale: Higher pain scores correlating with worse outcomes in mCRPC is well-established (e.g., incorporated in Halabi nomograms).

text: Higher fatigue_grade is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
rationale: Fatigue/performance status decrement predicting worse outcomes is standard prognostic knowledge.

text: Higher appetite_loss_grade is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
rationale: Anorexia/appetite loss as part of the cachexia syndrome predicting worse survival is established.

text: Lower bmi is associated with shorter pfs_months (positive correlation between bmi and pfs).
rationale: Low BMI/cachexia predicting worse cancer outcomes is well-established textbook knowledge.

text: Higher creatinine_mg_dl is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).

rationale: Coronary artery disease as a comorbidity adversely affecting cancer outcomes is established and not novel.

text: Patients with atrial_fibrillation=1 have shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Atrial fibrillation as part of cardiovascular comorbidity burden affecting cancer prognosis is established.

text: Patients with venous_thromboembolism_history=1 have shorter pfs_months.
rationale: VTE history is a well-known adverse prognostic factor in cancer (Khorana, Trousseau syndrome) and appears in guidelines.

text: Patients with autoimmune_disease=1 have different pfs_months than patients without.
rationale: Autoimmune disease impacting cancer outcomes (especially with immunotherapy) is a recognized clinical consideration.

text: Patients with depression_anxiety_diagnosis=1 have shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Depression/anxiety associated with worse cancer outcomes is a well-documented finding in supportive oncology literature.

text: Patients with prior_malignancy=1 have shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Prior malignancy as an adverse prognostic factor is well-established and routinely considered in oncology.

text: Mean pfs_months in race_ethnicity='black' patients differs from the rest of the cohort (test for racial disparity).
rationale: Racial disparities in cancer outcomes including PFS for Black patients are well-documented in oncology literature and guidelines.

text: Mean pfs_months in race_ethnicity='hispanic' patients differs from the rest of the cohort.
rationale: Ethnic disparities in cancer outcomes for Hispanic patients are established in oncology disparities literature.

text: Mean pfs_months in race_ethnicity='asian' patients differs from the rest of the cohort.
rationale: Differential outcomes by Asian race/ethnicity are a recognized topic in oncology disparities research.

text: Mean pfs_months in race_ethnicity='white' patients differs from the rest of the cohort.
rationale: Comparative outcomes for white patients versus other groups is a standard disparities analysis, not novel.

text: Uninsured patients (insurance_type='uninsured') have shorter pfs_months than insured patients.
rationale: Worse cancer outcomes among uninsured patients is a well-established finding in health services and oncology literature.

text: Medicaid patients have shorter pfs_months than other-insured patients.
rationale: Medicaid insurance is a known marker of worse cancer outcomes in established disparities research.

text: Privately-insured patients have longer pfs_months than other-insured patients.
rationale: Better outcomes among privately insured cancer patients is a well-documented disparity.

text: Medicare patients have different pfs_months than other-insured patients.
rationale: Differential outcomes by Medicare status is a standard, well-studied insurance-based disparity.

text: Patients with rural_residence=1 have shorter pfs_months than urban patients.
rationale: Rural-urban disparities in cancer outcomes are established in oncology disparities literature.

text: Higher education_years is associated with longer pfs_months.
rationale: Education/socioeconomic status correlating with better cancer outcomes is a well-established social determinant finding.

text: Higher smoking_pack_years is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Smoking pack-years correlating with worse outcomes is a well-established prognostic association in oncology guidelines.

text: At least one of the 25 candidate SNPs (snp_*) is associated with pfs_months at a Bonferroni-corrected significance threshold of 0.05/25 = 0.002.
rationale: A generic claim that at least one of 25 candidate SNPs reaches Bonferroni significance is a methodological/statistical assertion, not a novel biological insight beyond paradigm.

text: snp_rs4986893 (a CYP2C19 loss-of-function variant) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative direction per allele).
rationale: A CYP2C19 loss-of-function variant predicting shorter PFS in this oncology context is not part of standard guideline-level paradigm and represents a speculative pharmacogenomic hypothesis.

text: snp_rs429358 (APOE) shows a univariate association with pfs_months.
rationale: An APOE variant (rs429358) associating with cancer PFS is outside established oncology guideline paradigm and would be a novel cross-pathway claim.

text: After full multivariable adjustment for prognostic features and other treatments, the positive interaction of treatment_olaparib with brca2_mutation on pfs_months persists (refined predictive interaction).
rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA2-mutant disease is a guideline-established predictive interaction (PARP inhibitor sensitivity in BRCA-mutant tumors).

text: After full multivariable adjustment, no positive interaction of treatment_lu177_psma with psma_high on pfs_months emerges (confirming null from iter 3).
rationale: A null finding for Lu-177-PSMA Ã— PSMA-high interaction contradicts the established paradigm but as a negative result it is essentially a confirmation/replication question rather than a novel mechanistic claim.

text: After full multivariable adjustment, no positive interaction of treatment_pembrolizumab with msi_high on pfs_months emerges.
rationale: Pembrolizumab efficacy in MSI-high tumors is canonical guideline knowledge; reporting absence of this interaction is a null replication, not a novel hypothesis.

text: After full multivariable adjustment, no negative interaction of treatment_enzalutamide with ar_v7_positive on pfs_months emerges (AR-V7 does not behave as a predictive resistance biomarker in this cohort).
rationale: AR-V7 as a resistance biomarker to enzalutamide is established literature/guideline-adjacent knowledge; reporting a null is replication, not novel paradigm-breaking insight.

text: Gleason score becomes a significant negative prognostic factor for pfs_months after adjustment for the other prognostic features (despite null univariate effect).
rationale: A claim that Gleason score only emerges as prognostic after multivariable adjustment (suppressor effect with null univariate) is a non-obvious conditional/statistical claim beyond standard guideline framing.

text: ECOG, mCRPC, albumin, LDH, PSA, weight loss, visceral mets and Gleason score are each independently significant prognostic factors for pfs_months in the multivariable model (R^2 ~ 0.95).
rationale: ECOG, albumin, LDH, PSA, visceral metastases, weight loss, mCRPC status, and Gleason score being independent prognostic factors in prostate cancer is standard guideline/textbook knowledge.
